title: Silviana
notes:
A fun little disk-based action-RPG for the MSX2 similar to the Ys series.

Silviana is a cheerful girl living on a hill with her single mother. Upon her mother falling ill to an unknown condition and hearing rumor of a doctor Doppel having a cure-all medicine, she goes to ask him for help. Doppel has the medicine, but before he can give it out, he sends Silviana out to bring back his 8 treasures stolen by monsters.

This is an action game with some RPG elements. The world view highly resembles Zelda with one fixed screen at a time, scrolling to the next when you reach the edge. The game has a bump combat system borrowed from Ys, including the directional damage effect. The game does not have a leveling system. Instead, Silviana's attack and defense improve by accumulating money to buy better weapons and armors, and like Zelda, her health bar is expanded by finding hearts.

title: Tom Clancy's The Hunt for Red October (Book Version)
notes: In this adaptation of Tom Clancy's popular novel, you play the part of Captain Marko Ramius attempting to defect to the US. Once into the Atlantic, you must rendezvous with the US Navy and fake the destruction of the Red October. Your crew is not aware of the defection -- but the Soviet Navy is, and will try to stop you at any cost.

title: Young Sherlock: The Legacy of Doyle
notes: 1987 Japanese adventure game based on the 1985 film Young Sherlock Holmes, but with a completely different original story. It was developed by Pack-In-Video for the MSX home computer platform.
